summary: Fighting continues in southern Lebanon with IDF warnings against civilian return, while Hezbollah attacks Israeli forces with drones and rockets. Israel reports multiple lethal operations against Hamas, PIJ and Hezbollah militants in Gaza and Lebanon, and a tentative full cease‑fire agreement with Lebanon is announced. Regional actors, including Iran, signal potential escalation if Israeli strikes resume in Beirut.
key_points: IDF warns residents not to return to the area south of the Zahrani River as combat persists in southern Lebanon (Source: @amitsegal/61780).
Hezbollah FPV drone struck the vehicle of the Israeli Northern Command leader in southern Lebanon, causing no casualties (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/365762).

Israel and Lebanon reached a provisional full cease‑fire deal requiring Hezbollah to halt activities south of the Litani River and Israeli forces to withdraw to designated zones (Source: @almogboker78/17305).
IDF confirmed killing Hamas and PIJ operatives in Gaza, including the head of a PIJ rocket cell, Ahmed Abu Mughaysib (Source: @manniefabian/58392).
Two Hezbollah militants were eliminated on Mount Bofar by an IDF tank and supporting air strike (Source: @inon_yttach/8565).
Hezbollah launched six rockets toward Israeli positions in southern Lebanon after the cease‑fire announcement (Source: @naya_foriraq/76956).
Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi warned Iran would respond decisively if Israel attacks Beirut (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3312).

sections.executive_summary: Fighting continues in southern Lebanon with IDF warnings against civilian return, while Hezbollah attacks Israeli forces with drones and rockets. Israel reports multiple lethal operations against Hamas, PIJ and Hezbollah militants in Gaza and Lebanon, and a tentative full cease‑fire agreement with Lebanon is announced.

points: Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi stated Iran continues to study the new US proposal and will respond decisively if Israel attacks Beirut, indicating a threat of escalation (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3312).
IDF spokesperson reported alerts for hostile aircraft over the Aramsha area, indicating ongoing aerial threats (Source: @idf_telegram/24130).
IDF announced the killing of Hamas and PIJ operatives in Gaza, including PIJ rocket‑cell leader Ahmed Abu Mughaysib, highlighting continued offensive actions (Source: @manniefabian/58392).
Two Hezbollah militants were neutralized on Mount Bofar by a tank and air support, showing active ground engagements (Source: @inon_yttach/8565).
The Golani Brigade command changed hands at Beaufort Castle in southern Lebanon, reflecting command adjustments amid operations (Source: @manniefabian/58390).
IDF reported killing at least two Palestinian terror operatives in Gaza, including a PIJ rocket‑cell head, as part of ongoing counter‑terror operations (Source: @manniefabian/58392).
IDF warned civilians not to return to the south of the Zahrani River as combat persists, underscoring ongoing hostilities (Source: @amitsegal/61780).
Hezbollah FPV drone struck the vehicle of the Israeli Northern Command leader in southern Lebanon, though no injuries were reported (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/365762).
Hezbollah launched six rockets toward Israeli forces in southern Lebanon after the cease‑fire announcement (Source: @naya_foriraq/76956).
Israel and Lebanon agreed on a provisional full cease‑fire requiring Hezbollah to cease activity south of the Litani River and Israeli withdrawal to designated zones (Source: @almogboker78/17305).
No operational details about Syria or Iraq were present in the source messages.
No operational details about Gulf states were present in the source messages.

key_developments: IDF warns residents not to return to the area south of the Zahrani River as combat persists in southern Lebanon (Source: @amitsegal/61780).
Hezbollah FPV drone struck the vehicle of the Israeli Northern Command leader in southern Lebanon, causing no casualties (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/365762).

Israel and Lebanon reached a provisional full cease‑fire deal requiring Hezbollah to halt activities south of the Litani River and Israeli forces to withdraw to designated zones (Source: @almogboker78/17305).
IDF confirmed killing Hamas and PIJ operatives in Gaza, including the head of a PIJ rocket cell, Ahmed Abu Mughaysib (Source: @manniefabian/58392).
Two Hezbollah militants were eliminated on Mount Bofar by an IDF tank and supporting air strike (Source: @inon_yttach/8565).
Hezbollah launched six rockets toward Israeli positions in southern Lebanon after the cease‑fire announcement (Source: @naya_foriraq/76956).
Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi warned Iran would respond decisively if Israel attacks Beirut (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3312).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah continues to employ drones and rockets against Israeli forces in southern Lebanon, indicating a persistent low‑intensity threat despite cease‑fire talks. Israel's active targeting of Hamas, PIJ, and Hezbollah militants shows a broad counter‑terror focus across Gaza and Lebanon. Iran's warning of a decisive response to any Israeli strike on Beirut adds a strategic escalation risk, potentially expanding the conflict beyond the immediate border area.
sections.regional_response: Lebanese authorities appear to be negotiating a cease‑fire, conditioned on Hezbollah's cessation of hostilities south of the Litani River, while Israel maintains a security presence up to the 'yellow line' and seeks American backing for potential strikes in Beirut. Iran's vocal stance may embolden Hezbollah and complicate diplomatic efforts, and the United States' involvement remains ambiguous but influential in shaping Israeli operational decisions.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Hezbollah FPV drone activity and rocket launches is critical, as these tactics have proven effective against Israeli assets. The recent command change in the Golani Brigade may affect operational tempo in southern Lebanon. Continued IDF air and ground strikes against militant leaders in Gaza suggest an ongoing effort to degrade terrorist command structures. Diplomatic channels should be leveraged to solidify the tentative cease‑fire while preparing contingency plans for rapid escalation triggered by Iranian or Hezbollah actions.
